shortest_path.description: Given the graph (both as an incident list and visual representation), find the shortest path between two vertices.

parameters.description: Selected pair of nodes, provided as two integers representing the start and end nodes

standard.system_message: You are an expert in graph analysis, capable of working with both textual and visual representations of graphs. You will be provided with both an adjacency list and a visual representation of a graph. In the visual representation, nodes are represented as yellow circles with black borders each containing a unique integer label, nodes are ONLY connected if there is a visible black straight line drawn between them, the absence of a line means there is NO connection between those nodes. Provide direct answers without intermediate steps. Always format your output as: 'Path: [comma-separated nodes], Length: <number>'
standard.user_message: Please, find the shortest path between {start_node} and {end_node}. The length of the path is the number of edges traversed by the path. You may use both the incident list and visual representation to solve this task. Before submitting your final answer, verify that: 1) The path is valid by confirming each adjacent pair of nodes in your path is connected by an edge in the graph, and 2) The path is minimal by confirming no shorter valid path exists. Format your answer as 'Path: [comma-separated nodes], Length: <number>'

cot.system_message: You are an expert in graph analysis, capable of working with both textual and visual representations of graphs. You will be provided with both an adjacency list and a visual representation of a graph. In the visual representation, nodes are represented as yellow circles with black borders each containing a unique integer label, nodes are ONLY connected if there is a visible black straight line drawn between them, the absence of a line means there is NO connection between those nodes. Show your step-by-step reasoning process. Always format your output as: 'Path: [comma-separated nodes], Length: <number>'
cot.user_message: Please, find the shortest path between {start_node} and {end_node}. The length of the path is the number of edges traversed by the path. To solve this task, reason step by step using both the incident list and visual representation by: 1) Identifying all neighboring nodes of the start node, 2) Exploring possible paths systematically using breadth-first search, 3) Identifying when you've reached the end node, and 4) Verifying it's the shortest possible path. Before submitting your final answer, verify that: 1) The path is valid by confirming each adjacent pair of nodes in your path is connected by an edge in the graph, and 2) The path is minimal by confirming no shorter valid path exists. Format your final answer as 'Path: [comma-separated nodes], Length: <number>'

examples.task_solution: Selected Nodes: 17, 3. Here the steps of the analysis using both representations. From the incident list and visual graph, we can see that Node 17 has the following incident edges: 17,6 - 17,13 - 17,8 - 17,15. Node 6 has the following incident edges: 6,10 - 6,18 - 6,12 - 6,13. Node 13 has the following incident edges: 13,6 - 13,12 - 13,5 - 13,3 - 13,8. The visual representation confirms these connections and helps identify that the path through node 13 provides the shortest route. Therefore, the solution is Path: [17, 13, 3], Length: 2

Selected Nodes: 6, 8. Here the steps of the analysis using both representations. From the incident list and visual graph, we can see that Node 6 has the following incident edges: 6,16 - 6,12 - 6,5. Node 16 has the following incident edges: 16,5 - 16,2. Node 12 has the following incident edges: 12,9 - 12,13. Node 5 has the following incident edges: 5,13 - 5,20 - 5,16. Node 2 has the following incident edges: 2,1 - 2,3 - 2,15. Node 9 has the following incident edges: 9,13 - 9,17 - 9,8. The visual representation helps confirm that the path through nodes 12 and 9 provides the shortest route to node 8. Therefore, the solution is Path: [6, 12, 9, 8], Length: 3
